title: Bridge Essay: Solitary silence and social sounds: music can influence mental imagery, inducing thoughts of social interactions.
concept: Imagination as real perception — shared neural circuitry for imagery and perception, hallucination as continuum.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Imaginal Reconstruction** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Imagination as real perception — shared neural circuitry for imagery and perception, hallucination as continuum.

**Solitary silence and social sounds: music can influence mental imagery, inducing thoughts of social interactions.** - Scientific reports (2025). DOI: 10.1038/s41598-025-10309-2. PMID: 40730598. The COVID-19 pandemic was accompanied by a marked increase in the use of music listening for self-regulation. During these challenging times, listeners reported they used music 'to keep them company'; indicating that they may have turned to music for social solace. However, whether this is simply a figure of speech or an empirically observable effect on social thought that extends into mental imagery was previously unclear, despite its great potential for applications.
